analysis_description: Round-3 tapes (days 0–2): Black–Scholes implied volatility from option mid and VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid; delta and gamma per strike. TTE_days = 8 − tape_day_index per round3work/round3description.txt worked-example pattern (historical day +1→8d, +2→7d, +3→6d aligned to final Round-3 TTE=5d). T in years = TTE_days/365. Subsampled timestamps (step 500). Documented mean IV by strike/day and bid–ask spread (level 1) in summary JSON.
used_to_decide: Near-ATM strikes (5200/5300) show moderate mean IV (~0.23–0.24) and tight spreads vs wings; justified focusing execution on nearest strikes and extract hedge rather than wing IV noise. Equal-weight long strip mean gamma ~0.0092/day supports gamma-scalping thesis magnitude.

analysis_description: Backtester behavior: each historical day reloads Trader with empty traderData, so inferring tape_day from timestamp resets left TTE stuck at 8d for all days. Patched imc-prosperity-4-backtester/prosperity4bt/test_runner.py to inject __BT_TAPE_DAY__ (and __BT_ROUND__) into state.observations.plainValueObservations each tick; traders read this for TTE and day-specific rules. Re-ran all trader versions.
used_to_decide: Without correct tape_day, day-2 PnL and hedge tuning were misleading; after fix, tape day 2 extract hedging showed large negative contribution and v4 (no hedge on day 2) became best among gamma-focused variants.

analysis_description: Additional Greek thread on Round-3 tapes: computed BS-IV from mids for strikes 5100/5200/5300/5400 and summarized near-ATM vs farther strike bins by day (using TTE mapping from round3description pattern). Near-ATM options showed consistently higher average gamma (~0.00212, 0.00221, 0.00237 from day0->day2) than farther strikes (~0.00157, 0.00159, 0.00160), while average option delta stayed around 0.50. Also checked extract realized absolute move per tick by day (~0.85 on all days).
used_to_decide: This supported keeping focus on the two nearest strikes (gamma density) and testing inventory-hold / gamma-threshold sweeps rather than adding far-wing vouchers or hydrogel-heavy logic.

analysis_description: BS vega and theta (per calendar day) from implied vol solved on mids, for the two nearest strikes to extract at each subsampled tick (step 500). TTE 8/7/6 for tape days 0/1/2 per round3work/round3description.txt pattern. Outputs: vega_theta_summary.json (mean vega and mean |theta|/day by tape day) and vega_theta_sample.csv. Script: analyze_vega_theta.py.
used_to_decide: Mean BS vega on nearest two strikes falls from ~298 to ~254 as TTE shortens (days 0->2) while per-day |theta| rises ~4.3 to ~5.0, consistent with time decay building into shorter horizon. Suggested day-2 quote tightening (v8) but backtest with match-trades worse got zero VEV fills when MM_EDGE=0 (quotes at best bid/ask, no price improvement for taker). Day-2 requote speed sweep (v9) matched v4 PnL; keep v4 as simplest best.

analysis_description: IV vs extract co-movement: on each tape day, subsample every 100th timestamp in time order; compute BS implied vol from nearest-strike VEV mid and extract mid (TTE 8/7/6). Pearson correlation between successive differences d(IV) and d(S) on the subseries (n≈99 pairs/day). Script: analyze_iv_extract_comovement.py. Output: analysis_outputs/iv_deltaS_corr.json.
used_to_decide: Correlation is weak/mixed (day0 ~-0.04, days1-2 ~+0.28–0.31), so IV level from EMA is still a reasonable fair; no strong case to add an extract→IV overlay signal. Global requote speed sweep (v10) did not beat v4.

analysis_description: IV smile slice on the two strikes used for trading: at each subsampled timestamp (step 500), compute BS IV from mids for the two nearest strikes to extract; record skew = IV(K_high) - IV(K_low). Script analyze_two_strike_iv_skew.py; output two_strike_iv_skew.json (n=20 sample points per tape day).
used_to_decide: Mean IV of the higher near strike exceeds the lower by a few bp on average, increasing on later tape days (0: ~0.0003, 1: ~0.0031, 2: ~0.0044). Tried v11 to lean size toward higher instantaneous IV; total PnL 3444 < v4 3456, so this tilt is not adopted.

analysis_description: Greek carry and drift context for hedge policy: computed near-strike gamma/theta carry proxy on subsampled points (step 500) as (gamma_sum_two_nearest / |theta_day|_sum_two_nearest), with BS IV from mids and TTE 8/7/6 for days 0/1/2; compared with extract open-close drift. Day 1 and 2 show positive extract drift (+20.5, +28.0), while gamma/theta proxy is similar across days (~4.71e-4 to 4.92e-4), suggesting excess day-1 hedge intensity may hurt more than help.
used_to_decide: Motivated a targeted sweep to soften extract hedging on tape day 1 only (keep day 0 hedge and day 2 no-hedge). trader_v12 improved day-1 extract PnL from -726 (v4) to -610 and total PnL from 3456 to 3572.

analysis_description: Microstructure: mean level-1 spread (ask1-bid1) on the two VEV strikes nearest to extract mid, subsampled step 200. Script analyze_two_strike_spread.py; output two_strike_spread_by_day.json. Spreads are stable across tape days (~2.4–2.5 ticks), so MM edge=1 is not clearly forced tighter on any single day from spread alone.
used_to_decide: Continued day-1 extract hedge sweeps (parent v12). v13 further reduced day-1 hedge target and widened the deadband; backtest total rose to 4157 from v12=3572; adopt v13 as current best for this line.

analysis_description: Execution artifact analysis from saved full logs: ran v13 and v14 with --match-trades worse and --out to run_logs/*.log, plus captured stdout summaries. Parsed activity/tradeHistory into family12_outputs/run_fill_summary_v13_v14.json (num activity rows, trade rows, pnl by symbol, fill counts, absolute filled qty).
used_to_decide: v14 has nearly identical option-side fills and VEV PnL vs v13; the degradation is from extract PnL (v13 +156 vs v14 -140) due to over-softened day-1 hedge. Keep v13 (4157) as best in this family line.

analysis_description: Duplicate-check motivated variant shift: instead of another scalar day-1 hedge frac/band sweep, tested asymmetric day-1 hedge deadband around target_ex (wider when adding extract than when reducing extract). This is a distinct assumption from prior symmetric sweeps. Saved full run log and parsed fill summary for the run.
used_to_decide: v15 total 3792 (day1 795) is below v13 4157 (day1 1160), so asymmetric buffer did not beat the current best. Keep v13 as production candidate.

analysis_description: Priority response analysis: contract-by-contract extract move propagation to voucher mids + spread behavior (lead/lag, beta ladder, up/down asymmetry, spread after shocks). Script analyze_extract_voucher_response.py, output extract_voucher_response.json. Findings: near strikes show stable beta ladder (~0.45 for VEV_5200, ~0.27 for VEV_5300, ~0.12 for VEV_5400), weak lead/lag (mostly contemporaneous), and minimal spread widening after large extract moves (shock ratio ~1.0).
used_to_decide: Because response is contemporaneous, tested v16 day-1 clip reallocation (larger clip on the lower-delta of the two near strikes) vs equal clips. v16 scored 4150, slightly below v13 (4157). Superseded: v17 inverts the clip split toward the higher-delta (higher extract-beta) leg and improves total; see analysis iteration 11.

analysis_description: Follow-up to extract_voucher_response.json: the higher-delta near strike has larger dOption/dS (e.g. VEV_5200 ~0.43 vs VEV_5300 ~0.27 on day1), so v16 (larger clip on lower-delta) may underweight the leg that carries more local move per extract tick. v17 inverts v16 day1 clip split (6 on higher-delta, 4 on lower-delta). Parsed v13 vs v17 fills in run_fill_summary_v13_v17.json.
used_to_decide: v17 total 4167 (day1 1170) vs v13 4157 (day1 1160); extract PnL improves +39 (195 vs 156) with small VEV_5300 PnL tradeoff. Adopt v17 as new best; v16 remains suboptimal clip direction.

analysis_description: Regime test on extract continuation/reversal: computed corr between past and future extract moves over multiple windows; at 20-step horizon there is day-specific behavior (day1 weak momentum, day2 reversal). Tried mapping this into hedge_frac adaptation. v18 applied it on day1+day2 and blew up day2 extract PnL; v19 constrained adaptation to day1 only but remained below v17.
used_to_decide: Trend-conditioned hedge fraction was too fragile. Keep v17 as best; future changes should avoid day2 hedge adaptation and focus on richer option-side execution changes.

analysis_description: Near-strike L1 spread vs extract shocks: for each timestamp, if |dExtract|>=2 treat as shock, else baseline; take both nearest VEVs to S. JSON near_strike_spread_extract_shock.json. Mean spread barely differs baseline vs shock (~2.45-2.54 all days) — widening quotes on shocks is not supported by the tape, so v20 does not add spread-widen logic. Separately, day1-only 20-step extract continuation (positive past/future corr) motivated relaxing GAMMA_THRESH on day1 only in v20.
used_to_decide: v20 total ties v17 (4167); no PnL gain, so per tie-break keep simpler v17 as the preferred candidate. Shock-spread null result rules out a shock-widen policy for now.

analysis_description: Contract-level efficiency ranking: for each voucher, compute score = |beta(dOpt/dS)| * E|dS| / mean_spread using full-day tapes. Saved response_per_spread_rank.json. Across all days, ranking near ATM is stable: VEV_5200 > VEV_5100 > VEV_5300 > VEV_5000. Tested v21 using top2-by-score (5200+5100) rather than nearest pair.
used_to_decide: v21 total 3984 < v17 4167. Despite better score on 5100 than 5300, fixed top2 selection reduced day2 VEV_5200 PnL and did not improve extract hedge interaction. Keep nearest-pair structure in v17.

analysis_description: Shared 'joint tight book' thesis (round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt + outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt): when VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 both have L1 spread ≤2, forward extract mid (K=20 steps) is more favorable than when either is wide; P(both tight)≈0.10 on day 0 in the packaged summary. Merged origin/main: Round 3 now includes day_3 tape; TTE extended to 8/7/6/5 for historical days 0..3. Implemented gate logic on v20-class traders: v22 = hard no-VEV-MM unless joint tight; v23 = 0.5× clip when wide; v24 = floor(0.39×) wide clips to get integer separation from 5/6. Full logs + stdout for worse and all; parsed activity/tradeHistory via parse_backtest_log.py into family12_outputs/run_fill_summary_v22_v23_v24.json. Empirically, after the gamma entry filter, the joint spread gate is almost always on (5200+5300 are the quoted pair near spot), so v22–v24 match v20 total (4169 worse / 4065 all) and do not move toward the 200k/day extract+VEV floor.
used_to_decide: Keep v17/v20 class as the performance anchor; the STRATEGY.txt gate is better used as a **hypothesis** for future work that applies **before** the gamma filter, sizes extract **conditional** on gate, or widens the gate to wing strikes — current nearest-pair+gamma-threshold path rarely sees the 'wide' state at decision time. Next iteration should try gate-first sizing on extract and/or a lower gamma threshold in wide regime rather than gating the already-rare VEV quote path.
